what_it_does: Spawns the real company MCP server over stdio and walks an agent-native chain: initialize handshake, list tools, call sessions(op=search), and optionally post a consult intent via session_post, read it back via sessions(op='inbox'), then gather the found session into a channel, post to it, read back via channels describe/history, and disperse. Each step is checked and the probe exits non-zero on any failure.
observations: Uses subprocess.Popen with the real server argv .venv/bin/python mcp_face/server.py.
Implements a minimal newline-delimited JSON-RPC client class StdioMCP by hand.
Extracts tool result payloads from structuredContent or from content[0].text JSON.
Default run is read-only; --act performs real mailbox writes and channel lifecycle operations once.
Verifies the server advertises sessions and session_post tools and that the sessions schema exposes op='search'.
Verifies search results are live handles carrying state, primary_verb, and commands.
Applies a write-proof discipline: after posting it reads the same intent back via sessions(op='inbox').
The --act path also exercises channel_act gather, post, channels describe/history, and channel_act disperse.
Uses long timeouts ranging from 120 to 300 seconds.
Polls server stdout with select in a 1-second loop.
standouts: A real stdio MCP client that launches the actual production server argv rather than using stubs or an SDK.
Optional --act performs genuine writes and a full channel lifecycle, making it an integration test with side effects.
Self-checking probe: every protocol step has a PASS/FAIL assertion and loud failure.
conventions: Large module docstring maps each walked step to requirement labels R4.3/R4.4/R4.5.
CLI driven by argparse with a single --act flag and a -q query override.
Manual JSON-RPC framing via readline and select instead of an MCP client library.
Tool result extraction assumes FastMCP-style content[0].text JSON wrapping.
Inline comments narrate the chain and safety rationale throughout.
concerns: Hard-coded relative venv and server script paths make the probe brittle to environment layout changes.
tool_result_json assumes the payload is always in structuredContent.result or the first text content item; other shapes will raise.
If an exception occurs mid --act, the gathering may not be dispersed because disperse only runs after successful prior steps.
StdioMCP.close swallows all exceptions, which can hide cleanup problems.
The select polling loop wakes every second, which is simple but not the most responsive under heavy load.
notes: An end-to-end, self-checking MCP stdio probe that proves an external agent can discover, search, and optionally act on session handles through the real company server.
questions: What populates the transcript index so the default query reliably returns routable handles?
What is the exact server response shape when neither structuredContent nor a text content item is present?
Are concurrent --act runs safe, or can they race on the same gathering or mailbox?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is to prove the agent-native search-to-handle-to-act chain over the real MCP server, and the code does exactly that for read mode and, with --act, the full write/read-back/gather/disperse chain.
intention_signals: It is trying to demonstrate that an external agent can discover session search via the MCP tool schema, retrieve live session handles, durably act on them through the same face, and verify consequences by reading them back.
novelty: A programmatic MCP client that spawns the production server argv and exercises the complete search-to-handle-to-act-to-gather-to-disperse lifecycle in one executable probe.
contribution: A runnable, assertion-rich integration probe that validates the MCP stdio surface for session search, posting, inbox read-back, and momentary channel gathering without relying on UI or in-process shortcuts.
summary_for_embedding: A Python integration probe that launches the company MCP server over stdio, performs JSON-RPC initialize/tools/list/tools/call for session search, and optionally posts a consult intent and exercises a channel gather/disperse lifecycle with PASS/FAIL checks.
intention_for_embedding: To provide an agent-native, end-to-end test that the session search and action chain works through the real MCP stdio server.

symbols.does: Minimal stdio MCP client class that wraps a subprocess server and speaks newline-delimited JSON-RPC.
symbols.description: Initializes a Popen to the server argv, maintains a request id counter, and exposes request, notify, and close methods. It reads frames by polling stdout with select and parsing JSON lines.

symbols.does: Starts the MCP server subprocess and prepares stdio pipes.
symbols.description: Opens the server with stdin/stdout/stderr pipes in text mode, sets the working directory to ROOT, and initializes the request counter to zero.

symbols.does: Writes a JSON-RPC object followed by a newline and flushes stdin.
symbols.description: Serializes the given dict to JSON, appends a newline, and flushes it to the server subprocess's stdin.

symbols.does: Reads one newline-delimited JSON-RPC frame from server stdout with a timeout.
symbols.description: Polls stdout using select; if the server exits or closes stdout it raises RuntimeError with trailing stderr, otherwise parses the next non-empty JSON line.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON-RPC request and returns the matching result.
symbols.description: Increments the id, sends the request, then drains messages until the response with the same id arrives, raising on JSON-RPC errors.

symbols.does: Sends a JSON-RPC notification with no id.
symbols.description: Serializes a jsonrpc message without an id and flushes it to the server.

symbols.does: Closes stdin and terminates the server subprocess.
symbols.description: Closes stdin, attempts graceful terminate with wait, and falls back to kill if needed; exceptions are swallowed.

symbols.does: Extracts the JSON payload from an MCP tool result.
symbols.description: Prefers structuredContent.result, otherwise parses the first text content item's text as JSON; raises if no JSON payload is found.

symbols.does: Runs the agent-native MCP search-to-handle-to-act probe and prints PASS/FAIL for each step.
symbols.description: Parses CLI arguments, opens the stdio MCP client, performs initialize and tools/list, calls sessions(op=search), and optionally posts a consult intent, reads it back via inbox, and exercises a gather/post/disperse channel lifecycle; returns 0 only if all checks pass.

symbols.does: Local assertion helper that updates the global ok flag and prints a PASS/FAIL line.
symbols.description: Takes a label, condition, and optional extra detail; on failure it clears ok and may print extra context, otherwise prints PASS.
